summary.what: Post-commit note when stale-self commits auto-skip
summary.why: Phase 8 is the visibility safety net for the one failure mode agent-UX review identified as unrecoverable in-session: a marathon session running past the 24h window silently stale-skips its own work. Without surfacing, the agent never knows. The note doesn't block — that would defeat the reframe — but it preserves the signal that something was auto-skipped so the operator/agent can investigate.
summary.how: Restructured runPostCommitHook around a new classifyPostCommitState helper that walks ExplainPending and buckets reasons into actionable (in-session, the existing 'document this commit' nudge) and stale-self (the new note). Both surfaces fire independently and silently when their count is zero. The note explicitly references DefaultSessionWindow in its message so the operator sees what threshold tripped. Foreign-author skips deliberately stay silent — the operator already chose not to be that author. TIMBERS_SKIP_CROSS_AGENT_DEBT=1 short-circuits both surfaces.

notes: Considered listing the stale-self SHAs in the note — rejected because in long-running scenarios the list could grow large and noisy; the count + a pointer to --explain is the right balance. Considered rate-limiting (once per session) — deferred; the count-based note doesn't grow with repetition and the user's commit cadence naturally bounds how often it fires. ExplainPending walks the display range, which is the right scope (gate range is first-parent-strict; display is full-DAG and includes side-branch work that's relevant for visibility).
